title: Cleave systems, mountable cleave monitoring systems, and methods for separating bonded wafer structures
abstract: Cleave systems for separating bonded wafer structures, mountable cleave monitoring systems and methods for separating bonded wafer structures are disclosed. In some embodiments, the sound emitted from a bonded wafer structure is sensed during cleaving and a metric related to an attribute of the cleave is generated. The generated metric may be used for quality control and/or to adjust a cleave control parameter to improve the quality of the cleave of subsequently cleaved bonded wafer structures.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A cleave system for separating a bonded wafer structure, the system comprising: a cleaving device for cleaving the bonded wafer structure along a cleave plane; an acoustic sensor for sensing sound emitted from the bonded wafer structure during cleaving and to generate an output in response to the sensed sound; and a controller configured to generate a metric related to an attribute of the cleave based at least in part on the output of the acoustic sensor. 2. The cleave system as set forth in claim 1 wherein the metric is derived from an audio power profile generated during the cleave. 3. The cleave system as set forth in claim 2 wherein the metric is selected from the group consisting of delay between a cleave trigger and a start of cleave, duration of the cleave, mean power, maximum power, frequency at which maximum power occurs, standard deviation of power, magnitude of power oscillations, amount of cleave time below a threshold power, maximum single dip time below a threshold power and the number of power dips below a threshold power. 4. The cleave system as set forth in any one of claims 1 to 3 wherein the cleaving device is a mechanical cleaving device. 5. The cleave system as set forth in claim 4 wherein the mechanical cleaving device is configured to produce a propagated cleave of the bonded wafer structure. 6. The cleave system as set forth in any one of claims 1 to 3 wherein the cleaving device is a thermal cleaving device. 7. The cleave system as set forth in any one of claims 1 to 6 further comprising a trigger sensor for detecting when a cleave sequence has commenced for cleaving the bonded wafer structure. 8. The cleave system as set forth in any one of claims 1 to 7 further comprising an analog-to-digital converter for converting sound sensed by the acoustic sensor into data, the controller configured to generate a metric related to an attribute of the cleave based at least in part on the data generated by the analog to digital converter. 9. The cleave system as set forth in claim 8 wherein the analog-to-digital converter comprises a digitizer . 10. The cleave system as set forth in claim 8 wherein the analog-to-digital converter comprises a sound card . 11. The cleave system as set forth in any one of claims 1 to 10 wherein the controller is configured to generate 2 or more metrics related to attributes of the cleave based at least in part on the output of the acoustic sensor or is configured to generate 3 or more, 5 or more, 7 or more or 10 or more metrics related to attributes of the cleave based at least in part on the output of the acoustic sensor . 12. A mountable cleave monitoring system comprising : a bracket for mounting the system to a cleaving device for separating bonded wafer structures; an acoustic sensor for sensing sound emitted from a bonded wafer structure during cleaving, the acoustic sensor being mounted to the bracket; and a trigger sensor for detecting when a cleave sequence has commenced for cleaving the bonded wafer structure and to trigger recording of sound produced by the bonded wafer structure during the cleave, the trigger sensor being mounted to the bracket. 13. The mountable cleave monitoring system as set forth in claim 12 wherein the trigger sensor is a photoelectric sensor or a limit switch. 14. A method for separating bonded wafer structures, the method comprising: cleaving a bonded wafer structure; sensing sound emitted from the bonded wafer structure during cleaving; and generating a metric related to an attribute of the cleave based on sensed sound. 15. The method as set forth in claim 14 comprising adjusting a cleave control parameter to improve the quality of the cleave of subsequently cleaved bonded wafer structures. 16. The method as set forth in claim 14 or claim 15 wherein the metric is derived from the audio power profile generated during the cleave. 17. The method as set forth in claim 16 wherein the metric is selected from the group consisting of delay between a cleave trigger and a start of cleave, duration of the cleave, mean power, maximum power, frequency at which maximum power occurs, standard deviation of power, magnitude of power oscillations, amount of cleave time below a threshold power, maximum single dip time below a threshold power and the number of power dips below a threshold power. 18. The method as set forth in any one of claims 14 to 17 wherein the bonded wafer structure is cleaved mechanically. 19. The method as set forth in claim 18 wherein the bonded wafer structure is cleaved by propagation. 20. The method as set forth in any one of claims 14 to 19 wherein the bonded wafer structure is cleaved thermally . 21. The method as set forth in any one of claims 14 to 20 comprising detecting when a cleave sequence has commenced for cleaving the bonded wafer structure, wherein sound is sensed upon detecting that the cleave sequence has commenced . 22. The method as set forth in claim 21 wherein detecting when a cleave sequence has commenced comprises monitoring movement of a cleave arm. 23. The method as set forth in any one of claims 14 to 22 comprising converting sensed sound into data and generating a metric related to an attribute of the cleave based on sensed sound. 24. The method as set forth in any one of claims 14 to 23 wherein generating a metric related to an attribute of the cleave based on sensed sound comprises generating a power function by a fast Fourier transform algorithm . 25. The method as set forth in claim 24 wherein the algorithm is a stepped fast Fourier transform algorithm . 26. The method as set forth in any one of claims 14 to 24 wherein the generating a metric related to an attribute of the cleave based on sensed sound comprises generating a power function by digital filtering.
